text.title: GitHub Actions — Workflow Structure Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Multi-job workflows MUST declare explicit `needs:` relationships. Linear job sequences without DAG modelling are rejected when ≥3 jobs.
rule.rationale: DAG enables parallelism + clear dependencies; sequential YAML hides what could run concurrently.

rule.statement: Workflows MUST set `concurrency:` with a group key per context: PR groups cancel on new push; deployment groups serialize and don't cancel.
rule.rationale: Without concurrency control, multiple runs from the same PR pile up; deploys race each other.

rule.statement: Deployment jobs MUST use `environment:` with approvers + branch protection for production. Direct deploys without environment context are rejected.
rule.rationale: Environments are GHA's audit + approval surface; bypassing them removes the change-control trail.

rule.statement: Scheduled workflows MUST use UTC cron and SHOULD include `workflow_dispatch` for manual triggering during incidents.
rule.rationale: Local-time cron drifts with DST; missing dispatch leaves no escape hatch when nightly is broken.

rule.statement: Push/PR triggers MUST declare `branches:` / `paths:` filters. Implicit any-branch-any-path triggers waste runner budget and create unintended runs.
rule.rationale: Unfiltered triggers fire on doc-only changes and irrelevant branches; explicit filters keep CI focused.

rule.statement: Skip when single-job workflow OR all CI runs through another tool with GHA as hook only.
rule.rationale: Below threshold the structure ceremony is overhead; above threshold the rules are non-optional.
